strategy: Hit with Storm. He takes +5% from Storm and resists Myth 73%. His minions are wrapped in a -200% Brace ("Invulnerability") and can't be killed normally, so don't waste hits on them.
Expect three random minions on turn one; they get re-summoned when killed. Ignore them and tunnel the boss.

Ride the random cheat cycle. Every turn he throws two random AoE effects, one to each team. You might get +100% damage, +100% accuracy, or 4 pips, or eat -100% damage, -100% accuracy, 4-pip loss, or a flat 10% max-health hit. Keep blades stacked so a +100% damage round becomes your kill window, and re-blade if a -100% charm lands.
Plan for disruption. He Beguiles a random Wizard and can strip your setup. Protect the hitter's key blade with Aegis and keep a support able to hit if the main hitter gets Beguiled.

For the 62,125 health, run hitter, supports, and healer. Supports Feint and blade the hitter; the healer covers the 10% max-health chip and the 2,000 self-heals he gets from sacrificing his own minions. Burst him with one big Storm hit on a favorable cycle round.
cheatNotes: Latecomers get Confused for three rounds. Arrive on time.
He summons three random minions on turn one.
Killed minions come right back, so don't bother clearing them.
His minions are invulnerable and your damage does nothing to them. Focus the Writer only.
A minion killed and revived on the same turn briefly loses its invulnerability, but this isn't worth chasing.
When you kill the Writer his minions cast Furnace, so be ready to finish them or leave before it matters.
Every turn both teams get a random AoE effect. Some help you, some hurt, so build a buffer so a bad roll doesn't sink you.
A +100% Damage roll is your cue to hit. Stack blades so you can cash in immediately.
His side can heal 10% of health ignoring your infections, so expect the minions and boss to top up.
A -100% Damage roll can land on your team. Re-blade before hitting or your nuke does nothing.
A -100% Accuracy roll will fizzle your hit, so hold the finisher a round if it lands.
You can take a flat 10% max-health hit that no ward stops. Keep the team topped up.
He sacrifices his own minion to heal 2,000, so expect his health to tick back up if the fight drags.
He can Beguile a Wizard, turning them against the team. Protect the hitter's blades with Aegis and keep a backup hitter ready.
